Patch Set 2:

Jeff any chance to create a SLES12 patch?

Patch Set 2: Code-Review+2

Is this patch needed for the upstream kernel also? If yes, please send it to the linux-ext4@vger.kernel.org mailing list. 

One option for SLES12 is to just add this patch from RHEL7 to the SLES12 patch series and see if it compiles, since this code is fairly the same across different kernel versions. That should be done in a follow-on patch based on this one so that it does not restart the build and testing already completed on this patch.

Patch Set 2:

Andreas, patch is needed for upstream kernel, so sent it to the linux-ext4@vger.kernel.org mailing list. For SLES12 i will add this patch from RHEL7 to the SLES12 patch series.

Patch Set 4:

Since the patch is identical between RHEL7 and SLES12 then there is no need to make a duplicate copy of the patch for SLES12.  Instead, reference the rhel7/ext4-mmp.patch file from the ldiskfs-3.10-sles12.series file as is done with many other patches in that series file.

I didn't see this patch get submitted upstream.

Patch Set 6:

Can you abandon this patch in that case.

Patch Set 6:

don't understand why a whole new patch with a different Change-Id was created.  why not just rebase this patch and just make a fresh Patch Set?

Abandoned

Rebased the changes and uploaded here http://review.whamcloud.com/#/c/17841/, so this patch is abandoned

Patch Set 6:

Rather than abandoning an old patch and making a new version of the patch, it is better to rebase the patch and re-push it with the same Change-Id: tag.  Otherwise, it is much more difficult to compare the old and new versions of this patch against each other.

commit.subject: LU-6961 ldiskfs: buffer head leak in mmp
commit.message:
LU-6961 ldiskfs: buffer head leak in mmp

Release bh_check in case of error.
patch added for following kernel
RHEL7 3.10.0-229.14.1.el7
SLES11SP3 3.0.101-0.47.55
SLES12 - since the code is fairly same across RHEL7 versions
adding the patch from RHEL7 to the SLES12 patch series.

Seagate-bug-id: MRP-2337
Signed-off-by: Jadhav Vikram <jadhav.vikram@seagate.com>
Change-Id: I40863c95311eab5e7234decba983ab97d26db0ee
